target.title: fix(browser): accept authenticated DOM after auth probe 401/403
target.body:
## Summary

- prevent signed-in remote Chrome sessions from being rejected solely because cookie-only `/backend-api/me` returns 401 or 403
- retain failures for auth pages, visible login controls, composer-only shells, and missing profile/history authentication signals
- add focused 401/403 regression tests and document the resulting browser-mode behavior

Closes https://github.com/steipete/oracle/issues/241

This builds on the authenticated-DOM fallback introduced in https://github.com/steipete/oracle/pull/216. Thanks @hexsprite for the source reproduction and root-cause report.

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/pageActions.test.ts`
- run the repository’s broader check command after inspecting `package.json`
- perform applicable browser smoke coverage from `docs/manual-tests.md` with a real signed-in remote Chrome session; never click “Answer now”

ClawSweeper 🐠 replacement reef notes:
- Cluster: issue-steipete-oracle-241
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Credit @hexsprite and link https://github.com/steipete/oracle/issues/241 for the source reproduction and root-cause analysis.; Acknowledge the authenticated-DOM groundwork from https://github.com/steipete/oracle/pull/216.; Read the top of `CHANGELOG.md` before editing and group this user-facing browser-auth fix with related changes.; Follow `docs/manual-tests.md` for applicable browser smoke coverage; never click ChatGPT’s “Answer now” button.
- Validation: pnpm vitest run tests/browser/pageActions.test.ts
- Replacement reason: ClawSweeper could not update the source PR branch directly, so it opened a writable replacement PR instead.
